label: zal | come | ∅
sentence:
4 - Zal and Rodabeh

Anon it came about that Zal desired to see the kingdom.

label: he | set | ∅
sentence:
And he set forth, and there
followed after him a goodly train, and when they had journeyed a while they marched
with pomp into Cabul.

label: mihrab | be | ∅
sentence:
Now Mihrab, who was descended from Zohak the Serpent,
reigned in Cabul, yet he was worthy, prudent, and wise.

label: he | go | ∅
sentence:
When he heard that the son of
Sam, to whom he paid tribute, drew nigh unto the city, he went out to meet him, and his
nobles went with him, and slaves bearing costly gifts.

label: zal | prepare | feast
sentence:
And Zal, hearing that Mihrab was
at hand, prepared a feast in his tents, and Mihrab and his train feasted with him until the
night was far spent.

label: zal | praise | beauty
sentence: Now, after the King was gone, Zal praised his beauty.

label: noble | rise | ∅
sentence:
Then a noble
rose up and said unto him-

label: mouth | be | flower
sentence:
For
she is like unto the slender cypress, her face is brighter than the sun, her mouth is a
pomegranate flower.

label: zal | hear | word
sentence:
"
When Zal heard these words he was filled with longing, and sleep would not visit his
eyelids for thinking of her beauty.

label: he | open | door
sentence:
Now, when the day dawned, he opened the doors of his court, and the nobles stood about
him, each man according to his rank.

label: ∅ | come | mihrab
sentence:
And presently there came from Cabul Mihrab the
King to tender morning greeting to the stranger without his gates.

label: zal | desire | ∅
sentence:
And Zal desired that
Mihrab should crave a boon at his hands.

label: i | have | ∅
sentence:
Then spake Mihrab unto him saying-
"O ruler mighty and great, I have but one desire, and to bring it to pass is easy.

label: zal | say | he
sentence:
For I
crave thee that thou dwell as guest beneath my roof, and let my heart rejoice in thy
presence."
Then Zal said unto him, "O King, ask not this boon at my hands, I pray thee, for it can in
nowise be accomplished.

label: shah | anger | ∅
sentence:
The Shah and Sam would be angered should they learn that I
had eaten under the roof of Zohak.

label: i | beg | thee
sentence: I beg of thee ask aught but this.

label: mihrab | hear | word
sentence:
"
When Mihrab heard these words he was sorrowful, and bent low before Zal, and departed
from out the tents.

label: eye | look | he
sentence: And the eye of Zal looked after him, and yet again he spake his praises.

label: he | bethink | he
sentence:
Then he bethought him of the King's daughter, and how that she was fair, and he was
sunk in brooding and desire, and the days passed unheeded over his head.

label: it | come | ∅
sentence:
Now it came to pass that on a certain morning Mihrab stepped forth from his palace to
the house of the women to visit Sindokht his wife, and her daughter Rodabeh.

label: house | be | garden
sentence:
Truly the
house was like to a garden for colour and perfume, and over all shone those moons of
beauty.

label: he | marvel | loveliness
sentence:
Now when Mihrab had greeted Rodabeh he marvelled at her loveliness, and
called down the blessings of Heaven upon her head.

label: sindokht | open | lip
sentence:
Then Sindokht opened her lips and
questioned Mihrab concerning the stranger whose tents were without their gates.

label: she | pray | thee
sentence:
And she
said-
"I pray thee tell unto me what manner of man is this white-haired son of Sam, and is he
worthy the nest or the throne?

label: mihrab | say | she
sentence:
"
Then Mihrab said unto her, "O my fair cypress, the son of Sam is a hero among men.

label: heart | be | lion
sentence:
His
heart is like unto a lion's, his strength is as an elephant's, to his friends he is a gracious
Nile, unto his enemies a wasting crocodile.

label: ∅ | be | he
sentence:
And in him are even blemishes turned to
beauties, his white locks but enhance his glory.

label: rodabeh | listen | word
sentence:
"
When Rodabeh had listened to these words her heart burned with love for Zal, so that she
could neither eat nor rest, and was like unto one that hath changed her shape.

label: she | tell | secret
sentence:
And after a
while, because that she could bear the burden thereof no longer, she told her secret to the
slaves that loved and served her.

label: she | charge | ∅
sentence:
And she charged them tell no man, and entreated of
them that they would aid her to allay the troubles of her heart.

label: they | fill | fear
sentence:
And when the slaves had
listened to her story, they were filled with fear, and with one accord entreated her that she
would dismiss from her heart one branded among men, and whom his own father had cast
out.

label: rodabeh | listen | voice
sentence: But Rodabeh would not listen to their voice.

label: they | cast | ∅
sentence:
And when they beheld that she was firm
in her spirit, and that their words were vain, they cast about how they might serve her.

label: it | do | desire
sentence:
And one among them who was wise above the rest opened her lips and spake-
"O moon-faced beauty, slender cypress, it shall be done at thy desire.

label: ∅ | plant | tree
sentence:
"
Then Rodabeh was glad and said-
"An the issue be happy, there shall be planted for thee a noble tree, and it shall bear
riches and jewels, and wisdom shall cull its fruits."

label: slave | ponder | heart
sentence:
Then the slaves pondered in their hearts how they should compass their end, for they
knew that only by craft could it be brought about.

label: they | clothe | themselves
sentence:
Straightway they clothed themselves in
costly raiment, and went forth blithely into the garden of flowers that was spread beside
the river's bank without the city.

label: they | gather | rose
sentence:
And they gathered roses, and decked their hair with
blossoms, and threw them into the stream for sooth-telling; and as they gathered they
came unto the spot over against which were pitched the tents of Zal.

label: they | be | slave
sentence: "They are slaves sent forth by the moon of Cabul into the garden of flowers.

label: heart | leap | joy
sentence:
"
Now when Zal heard this his heart leaped for joy, and he set forth unto the river's bank
with only one page to bear him company.

label: he | take | bow
sentence:
And seeing a water-bird fly upwards, he took
his bow and shot it through the heart, and it fell among the rose-gatherers.

label: bade | cross | water
sentence:
Then Zal bade
the boy cross the water and bring him the bird.

label: ∅ | tell | name
sentence:
And when he had landed, the moon-faced
women pressed about him and questioned him, saying-
"O youth, tell us the name of him who aimeth thus surely, for verily he is a king among
men.

label: answering | say | ∅
sentence:
"
Then the boy answering said, "What!

label: ∅ | know | ∅
sentence: know

label: world | hath | ∅
sentence:
The world
hath not his equal for strength and beauty.

label: girl | reprove | he
sentence:
"
But the girls reproved him, and said, "Not so, boast not thus vainly, for the house of
Mihrab holdeth a sun that o'ershines all besides.

label: page | smile | ∅
sentence:
"
And the page smiled, and the smile yet lingered on his lips when he came back to Zal.

label: they | speak | what
sentence:
What have they spoken unto thee that thou openest thy lips and
showest thy ivory teeth?

label: boy | tell | speech
sentence:
"
Then the boy told unto him the speech of the women.

label: ∅ | go | ∅
sentence:
And Zal said-
"Go over yet again and bid them tarry, that they may bear back jewels with their roses.

label: he | choose | ∅
sentence:
"
And he chose forth from among his treasures trinkets of pearl and gold, and sent them to
the slaves.

label: one | crave | ∅
sentence:
Then the one who had sworn to serve Rodabeh above the rest craved that she
might look upon the face of the hero, for she said-
"A secret that is known to three is one no longer."

label: zal | grant | desire
sentence:
And Zal granted her desire, and she told him of Rodabeh and of her beauty, and his
passion burned the more.

label: i | pray | thee
sentence:
And he spake-
"Show unto me, I pray thee, the path by which I may behold this fair one, for my heart is
filled with longing.

label: ∅ | suffer | ∅
sentence:
"
Then the slave said-
"Suffer that we go back to the house of the women, and we will fill the ears of Rodabeh
with praises of the son of Sam, and will entangle her in the meshes of our net, and the
lion shall rejoice in his chase of the lamb."

label: bade | go | ∅
sentence:
Then Zal bade her go forth, and the women returned to the house rejoicing and saying-
"The lion entereth the snare spread forth to entrap him, and the wishes of Rodabeh and
Zal will be accomplished."

label: they | still | wrath
sentence: But they stilled his wrath and came unto where Rodabeh awaited them.

label: they | tell | she
sentence:
And they
told her of Zal, the son of Sam, and of his beauty and his prowess.

label: rodabeh | smile | ∅
sentence:
And Rodabeh smiled
and said-
"Wherefore have ye thus changed your note?

label: ye | spake | while
sentence:
For a while back ye spake with scorn of this
bird-reared youth, on whose head hang the locks of a sage, but now are ye loud in his
praises."

label: rodabeh | begin | ∅
sentence: Then Rodabeh began privily to deck her house that it might be worthy a guest.

label: she | hang | it
sentence:
With
brocades of Roum and carpets of Ind did she hang it, and she perfumed it with musk and
ambergris, and flowers did she cause to bloom about the rooms.

label: slave | go | zal
sentence:
And when the sun was
sunk, and the doors of the house were locked and the keys withdrawn, a slave went forth
unto Zal, the son of Sam.

label: she | spake | he
sentence:
And she spake unto him in a low voice-
"Come now, for all is ready.

label: zal | follow | she
sentence:
"
And Zal followed after her.

label: they | come | house
sentence:
And when they were come to the house of the women Zal
beheld the daughter of the King standing upon the roof, and her beauty was like unto a
cypress on which the full moon shineth.

label: she | spake | ∅
sentence:
And when she beheld him, she spake and said-
"I bid thee welcome, O young man, son of a hero, and may the blessing of Heaven rest
upon thee."

label: zal | answer | benison
sentence:
And Zal answered her benison, and prayed that he might enter into nearer converse, for
he was on the ground and she was on the roof.

label: faced | loosen | tress
sentence:
Then the Peri-faced loosened her tresses,
and they were long, so that they fell from the battlements unto the ground.

label: she | say | zal-
sentence:
And she said
unto Zal-
"Here hast thou a cord without flaw.

label: mount | be | ∅
sentence:
Mount, O Pehliva, and seize my black locks, for it is
fitting that I should be a snare unto thee.

label: zal | cry | ∅
sentence:
"
But Zal cried, "Not so, O fair one, it would beseem me ill to do thee hurt.

label: he | cover | hair
sentence:
"
And he covered her hair with kisses.

label: he | call | cord
sentence:
Then he called for a cord and made a running knot,
and threw it upwards and fastened it to the battlements.

label: he | swing | himself
sentence:
And with a bound he swung
himself upon the roof.

label: rodabeh | take | hand
sentence:
Then Rodabeh took his hand and they stepped down together into
the golden chambers, and the slaves stood round about them.

label: they | gaze | other
sentence:
And they gazed upon each
other and knew that they excelled in beauty, and the hours slipped by in sweet talk, while
love was fanned in their hearts.

label: cried- | anger | ∅
sentence:
Then Zal cried-
"O fair cypress, musk-perfumed, when Minuchihr shall learn of this he will be angered
and Sam also will chide.

label: they | say | ∅
sentence:
And they will say I have forgotten my God, and will lift their
hands against me.

label: i | swear | thee
sentence:
But I swear unto thee that this life is to me vile if it be not spent in thy
presence.

label: i | call | heaven
sentence:
And I call upon Heaven to hear me that none other but thee will I call my
bride.

label: i | swear | oath
sentence:
"
And Rodabeh said-
"I too will swear unto thee this oath.

label: hour | speed | ∅
sentence:
"
So the hours sped, and there arose from out the tents of the King the sound of drums that
announce the coming of the day.

label: ∅ | cry | zal
sentence:
Then cried Zal and Rodabeh of one accord-
"O glory of the world, tarry yet a while, neither arrive so quickly.

label: sun | give | ear
sentence:
"
But the sun gave no ear to their reproaches, and the hour to part was come.

label: zal | swing | himself
sentence:
Then Zal
swung himself from the battlements unto the ground, and quitted the house of his beloved.

label: he | call | mubid
sentence:
Now when the earth was flooded with light, and the nobles and chiefs had tendered unto
Zal their morning greetings as was their wont, he called about him his Mubids, and laid
before them how that he was filled with love for a daughter of the Serpent.

label: mubid | be | ∅
sentence:
And the
Mubids when they heard it were troubled, and their lips were closed, and the words were
chained upon their tongues.

label: ∅ | be | none
sentence:
For there was none of them that listed to mingle poison in the
honey of this love.

label: zal | reprove | they
sentence:
Whereupon Zal reproved them, and said that he would bestow on
them rich gifts if they would open their mouths.

label: they | spake | ∅
sentence:
Then they spake and said unto him that
the honour of a king could not suffer by a woman, and though Mihrab be indeed of
Zohak's race, he was noble and valiant.

label: they | urge | he
sentence:
And they urged him to write unto his father and
crave Sam to wait upon the Shah.

label: zal | call | scribe
sentence: Then Zal called unto him a scribe and bade him write down the words that he spake.

label: he | tell | love
sentence:
And
he told unto Sam his love and his fears.

label: he | recall | he
sentence:
And he recalled unto him how that he had cast
him out, and how that he had lived in a nest, and a bird had reared him, and the sun had
poured down upon his head, and raw flesh had been his nourishment the while his father
had sat within a goodly house clothed in silk.

label: he | recall | promise
sentence:
And he recalled the promise given to him
by Sam.

label: neither | seek | ∅
sentence: Neither did he seek to justify that which was come about.

label: he | give | letter
sentence:
Then he gave the letter
to a messenger, and bade him ride until he should be come into the presence of Sam.

label: sam | hear | ∅
sentence:
When Sam had heard the words of his son his spirit was troubled, and he cried-
"Woe unto me, for now is clear what hath so long been hidden.

label: he | ponder | ∅
sentence:
"
And he pondered long what he should answer.

label: i | break | oath
sentence:
For he said, "If I say, Abandon this desire,
sow no discord, return to reason, I break my oath and God will punish me.

label: offspring | come | ∅
sentence:
Yet if I say,
Thy desire is just, satisfy the passions of thy heart, what offspring can come to pass from
the union of a Deev and the nursling of a bird?

label: heart | be | ∅
sentence:
"
And the heart of Sam was heavy with care.

label: he | call | mubid
sentence:
So he called unto him his Mubids that they
should search the stars, for he said-
"If I mingle fire and water I do ill, and ill will come of it.

label: men | search | secret
sentence:
"
Then all that day the Wise Men searched the secrets of Fate, and they cast the horoscope
of Zal and Rodabeh, and at even they returned to the King rejoicing.

label: they | find | ∅
sentence:
And they found him
torn with anguish.

label: hail | follow | movement
sentence:
Then they said-
"Hail unto thee, O Sam, for we have followed the movement of the stars and counted
their course, and we have read the message of the skies.

label: son | bear | zal
sentence:
And it is written, 'A clear spring
shall issue into the day, a son shall be born to Zal, a hero full of power and glory, and
there shall not be his like in Iran.'

label: soul | be | ∅
sentence:
"
Now when Sam had drunk in these words, his soul was uplifted, and he poured gifts upon
the Mubids.

label: he | call | messenger
sentence:
Then he called to him the messenger of Zal, and he gave him pieces of silver,
and bade him return unto his master and say-

label: i | hold | ∅
sentence:
"I hold thy passion folly, O my son, but because of the oath that I have sworn to thee it
shall be done at thy desire.

label: i | hie | i
sentence: I will hie me unto Iran and lay thy suit before the Shah.

label: sam | call | army
sentence:
"
Then Sam called together his army and set forth for Iran, and the sound of trumpets and

cymbals went before him.

label: he | rejoice | ∅
sentence:
Now when the messenger was come back to Zal, he rejoiced and praised God, and gave
gold and silver to the poor, and gifts unto his servants.

label: he | close | eye
sentence:
But when night was come he
could not close his eyes in slumber, nor could he rest during the day.

label: neither | drink | wine
sentence:
Neither did he drink
wine nor demand the singers, for his soul was filled with longing after his love.

label: ∅ | come | slave
sentence:
And
presently there came out to him a slave, and he gave unto her Sam's letter that she might
bear it to Rodabeh.

label: rodabeh | rejoice | ∅
sentence:
And Rodabeh rejoiced also, and chose from among her treasures a
costly crown and a ring of worth, and bade the woman bear them unto Zal.

label: she | meet | sindokht
sentence:
Now as she
quitted the chamber she met Sindokht.

label: queen | question | she
sentence:
And the Queen questioned her and said-
"Whence comest thou?

label: ∅ | seek | thou
sentence:
Reply to all my questions, neither seek thou to deceive me, for
already a long time do I suspect thy passing to and fro.

label: woman | tremble | ∅
sentence:
"
And the woman trembled as she heard these words, and fell down and kissed the feet of
the Queen, and said-
"Have pity on thine handmaiden, who is poor and gaineth her bread as she can.

label: i | go | house
sentence:
I go into
the houses of the rich and sell to them robes and jewels.

label: rodabeh | hath | ∅
sentence:
And Rodabeh hath this day
bought of me a tiara and a bracelet of gold.

label: sindokht | say | ∅
sentence:
"
Then said Sindokht, "Show unto me the money thou hast received for the same, that my
anger be appeased.

label: woman | answer | ∅
sentence:
"
And the woman answered and said, "Demand not that I show unto thee that which I have
not, for Rodabeh will pay me to-morrow.

label: sindokht | know | ∅
sentence:
"
Now Sindokht knew that these words were feigned, and she searched the sleeve of the
woman, and lo!

label: she | find | tiara
sentence: she found therein the tiara that Rodabeh had broidered with her hands.

label: she | anger | ∅
sentence: Then she was angered, and commanded that the slave should be bound in chains.

label: she | desire | ∅
sentence:
And she
desired that her daughter be brought into her presence.

label: sindokht | open | mouth
sentence:
And when she was come,
Sindokht opened her mouth and spake, saying-
"O moon of noble race, to whom hath been taught naught but that which is good, how
hast thou gone astray upon the paths of evil?

label: ∅ | cometh | woman
sentence: From whom cometh this woman?

label: man | destine | gift
sentence:
For what man are destined thy gifts?"
When she had heard, Rodabeh was abashed, but after a while she told all unto Sindokht.

label: she | fear | wrath
sentence:
Now when the Queen had heard she was confounded, for she feared the wrath of the
Shah, and that he would raze Cabul to the dust for this mischance.

label: she | go | room
sentence:
And she went into her
rooms and wept in her sorrow.

label: mihrab | come | sindokht
sentence:
Then presently Mihrab the King came in to Sindokht, and
he was of joyful mind, for Zal had received him graciously.

label: he | question | grief
sentence:
But when he beheld her tears
he questioned of her grief.

label: she | tell | he
sentence:
Then she told him how that his daughter was filled with love
for Zal, the son of Sam.

label: heart | be | ∅
sentence:
And when Mihrab had heard her to an end, his heart also was
troubled, for he knew that Cabul could not stand before the Shah.

label: minuchihr | be | ∅
sentence:
Minuchihr, too, when he had heard these things, was troubled, for he beheld in them the
device of Ahriman, and feared lest this union should bring evil upon Iran.

label: he | bid | sam
sentence:
And he bade
Nauder call Sam before him.

label: he | spake | ∅
sentence:
Now when Sam heard the desire of the Shah, he spake and
said-
"I obey, and the sight of the King will be a banquet.

label: sam | go | presence
sentence:
"
Then Sam went into the presence of Minuchihr, and he kissed the ground, and called
down blessings upon the head of the Shah.

label: minuchihr | raise | he
sentence:
But Minuchihr raised him and seated him
beside him on the throne, and straightway began to question him concerning the war, and
the Deevs of Mazinderan.

label: sam | tell | story
sentence: Then Sam told him all the story of his battles.

label: he | praise | prowess
sentence:
And Minuchihr
listened with joy though the tale was long, and when Sam had ended he praised his
prowess.

label: he | lift | crown
sentence:
And he lifted his crown unto heaven and rejoiced that his enemies were thus
confounded.

label: ∅ | be | ∅
sentence:
Then be bade a banquet be spread, and all night long the heroes feasted and
shortened the hours with wine.

label: curtain | open | ∅
sentence:
But when the first rays of morn had shed their light, the
curtains of the Shah's house were opened, that he might hold audience and grant the
petitions of his people.

label: sam | come | first
sentence:
And Sam the Pehliva came the first to stand before the King, for
he desired to speak to him of Zal.

label: shah | suffer | he
sentence:
But the Shah of the world would not suffer him to open
his lips, but said unto him-
"Go hence, O Sam, and take with thee thine army, for I command thee to go yet again to
battle.

label: ∅ | set | cabul
sentence:
Set forth unto Cabul and burn the house of Mihrab the King, and utterly destroy
his race and all who serve him, nor suffer that any of the seed of Zohak escape
destruction, for I will that the earth be delivered of this serpent brood.

label: sam | hear | word
sentence:
"
When Sam heard these words he knew that the Shah was angered, and that speech would
avail him naught.

label: he | kiss | throne
sentence:
So he kissed the throne and touched the earth with his forehead, and
said, "Lord, I am thy servant, and I obey thy desires."

label: he | depart | ∅
sentence:
And he departed, and the earth
trembled under the stamping of footmen and of hoofs, and the air of the city was
darkened with his spears.

label: news | reach | cabul
sentence:
Now the news of Sam's intent reached even unto Cabul, and the land was sunk in woe,
and weeping filled the house of the King.

label: zal | be | wroth
sentence:
But Zal was wroth, and he went forth to meet
his father.

label: he | crave | audience
sentence:
And when he was come to the spot where he had encamped his army, he
craved an audience.

label: sam | grant | it
sentence:
And Sam granted it, and Zal reminded him yet again of his oath, and
desired that he would spare the land of Cabul, nor visit his judgments upon the innocent.

label: heart | move | ∅
sentence:
When Sam had listened, his heart was moved, and he said-
"O my son, thou speakest that which is right.

label: i | be | thee
sentence:
To thee have I been unjust from the day of
thy birth.

label: i | find | remedy
sentence:
But stay thy wrath, for surely I will find a remedy, and thy wishes shall yet be
accomplished.

label: zal | kiss | ground
sentence:
For thou shalt bear a letter unto the Shah, and when he shall have looked
on thy face, he will be moved with compassion and cease to trouble thee."
Then Zal kissed the ground before his father and craved the blessings of God upon his
head.

label: sam | dictate | letter
sentence:
And Sam dictated a letter to the Shah, and he spoke therein of all he had done for
Minuchihr, and how he had killed the dragon that had laid waste the land, how he had
ever subdued the foes of Iran, and how the frontiers were enlarged by his hands.

label: he | wax | ∅
sentence:
Yet now
was he waxing old, and could no longer do doughty deeds.

label: son | be | ∅
sentence:
But a brave son was his,
worthy and true, who would follow in his footsteps.

label: heart | devour | love
sentence:
Only his heart was devoured of love,
and perchance he would die if his longing were unsatisfied.

label: he | commend | wisdom
sentence:
And therewith he
commended to the wisdom of the Shah the affairs of Zal.

label: zal | set | it
sentence:
When the letter was ended Zal set forth with it unto the court, and the flower of his army
went with him.

label: fear | be | cabul
sentence:
But the fear of Minuchihr was great in Cabul, and Mihrab pondered how he should
quench the wrath of the King of kings.

label: he | spake | sindokht
sentence:
And he spake to Sindokht and said-
"For that the King is angered against me because of thee and thy daughter, and because I
cannot stand before him, I will lead Rodabeh unto his court and kill her before his eyes.

label: anger | allay | ∅
sentence: Perchance his anger may be thus allayed."

label: sindokht | listen | word
sentence:
Sindokht listened to his words in silence, and when he had ended she cast about her for a
plan, for she was quick of wit.

label: she | come | presence
sentence:
And when she had found one she came again into the
presence of Mihrab, and she craved of him that he should give her the key of his treasury.

label: it | be | ∅
sentence:
For she said-
"This is not the hour to be strait-handed; suffer that I take what seemeth good unto me
and go before Sam, it may be that I move him to spare the land.

label: mihrab | agree | demand
sentence:
"
And Mihrab agreed to her demand because of the fear that devoured him.

label: sindokht | go | house
sentence:
Then Sindokht
went out to the house of Sam, and she took with her three hundred thousand pieces of
gold, and sixty horses caparisoned in silver, bearing sixty slaves that held cups filled to
the brim with musk and camphor, and rubies, and turquoise, and precious stones of every
kind.

label: ∅ | follow | dromedary
sentence:
And there followed two hundred dromedaries and four tall Indian elephants laden
with carpets and brocades of Roum, and the train reached for two miles beyond the
King's gates.

label: she | bid | guardian
sentence:
Now when Sindokht was come to Seistan she bade the guardians of the door
say unto Sam that an envoy was come from Cabul bearing a message.

label: sam | grant | audience
sentence:
And Sam granted
an audience, and Sindokht was brought into his presence.

label: she | kiss | ground
sentence:
Then she kissed the ground at
his feet and called upon Heaven to shower down blessings on his head.

label: she | cause | ∅
sentence:
And when she
had done so, she caused her gifts to be laid before Sam, and when Sam beheld these
treasures, he marvelled and thought within himself, "How cometh it that a woman is sent
as envoy from a land that boasteth such riches?

label: shah | anger | ∅
sentence:
If I accept them the Shah will be angered,
and if I refuse perchance Zal will reproach me that I rob him of his heritage."

label: he | lift | head
sentence:
So he lifted
his head and said-
"Let these treasures be given unto the treasurer of my son."

label: she | rejoice | ∅
sentence:
When Sindokht beheld that her gifts were accepted, she rejoiced and raised her voice in
speech.

label: ∅ | answer | sam
sentence:
And she questioned Sam, saying-
"Tell me, I pray thee, what wrong have the people of Cabul done unto thee that thou
wouldst destroy them?"
Then answered Sam the hero, "Reply unto my questions and lie not.

label: art | thou | slave
sentence:
Art thou the slave or
the wife of Mihrab, and is it thy daughter whom Zal hath seen?

label: i | know | ∅
sentence:
If indeed it be so, tell me,
I pray, of her beauty, that I may know if she be worthy of my son.

label: sindokht | say | ∅
sentence:
"
Then Sindokht said, "O Pehliva, swear to me first a great oath that thou wilt spare my life
and the lives of those dear unto me.

label: i | recount | ∅
sentence:
And when I am assured of thy protection I will
recount all that thou desirest.

label: sam | take | hand
sentence:
"
Then Sam took the hand of Sindokht, and he sware unto her a great oath, and gave her his
word and his promise.

label: she | be | ∅
sentence:
And when she had heard it she was no longer afraid, and she told
him all her secrets.

label: i | be | race
sentence:
And she said-
"I am of the race of Zohak, and wife unto the valiant Mihrab, and mother of Rodabeh,
who hath found favour in the eyes of thy son.

label: i | come | ∅
sentence:
And I am come to learn of thy desire, and
who are thine enemies in Cabul.

label: i | pray | thee
sentence:
Destroy the wicked, and those who merit chastisement,
but spare, I pray thee, the innocent, or thy deeds will change day into night."

label: ∅ | spake | sam
sentence:
Then spake Sam, "My oath is sacred, and if it cost my life, thou and thine and Cabul may
rest assured that I will not harm them.

label: i | desire | ∅
sentence:
And I desire that Zal should find a wife in
Rodabeh, though she be of an alien race."

label: he | tell | she
sentence:
And he told her how that he had written to the Shah a letter of supplication such as only
one in grief could pen, and how Zal was absent with the message, and he craved her to
tell him of Rodabeh.

label: sindokht | reply | ∅
sentence:
But Sindokht replied, "If the Pehliva of the world will make the hearts of his slaves
rejoice, he will visit us and look with his own eyes upon our moon.

label: sam | smile | ∅
sentence:
And Sam smiled and said, "Rest content and deliver thine heart of cares, for all shall end
according unto thy desires.

label: she | bid | he
sentence:
"
When Sindokht heard this she bade him farewell and made all haste to return.

label: sam | load | she
sentence:
And Sam
loaded her with gifts and bade her depart in peace.

label: face | shine | ∅
sentence:
And Sindokht's face shone brightly,
like unto the moon when she hath been eclipsed, and hope once more reigned in her
breast.

label: ∅ | listen | ∅
sentence: Now listen to what happened to Zal while these things were passing in Seistan.

label: he | come | court
sentence:
When he
was come to the court of Minuchihr he hastened into his presence, and kissed the ground
at his feet, and lay prostrate before him in the dust.

label: he | move | ∅
sentence:
And when the Shah saw this he was
moved, and bade his servants raise Zal, and pour musk before him.

label: zal | draw | nigh
sentence:
Then Zal drew nigh
unto the throne and gave to the King the letter written by Sam the son of Neriman.

label: hath | awaken | pain
sentence:
And
when Minuchihr had read it he was grieved, and said-
"This letter, written by Sam thy father in his sorrow, hath awakened an old pain within
me.

label: i | do | that
sentence: But for the sake of my faithful servant I will do unto thee that which is thy desire.

label: i | ask | ∅
sentence: Yet I ask that thou abide with me a little while that I may seek counsel about thee."

label: cook | bring | table
sentence:
Then the cooks brought forth a table of gold, and Zal was seated beside the Shah and all
the nobles according to their rank, and they ate flesh and drank wine together.

label: ∅ | spring | steed
sentence:
Then when
the mantle of night was fallen over the earth Zal sprang upon his steed and scoured the
land in the unrest of his spirit, for his heart was full of thoughts and his mouth of words.

label: he | present | himself
sentence: But when morning was come he presented himself before the Shah in audience.

label: speech | find | favour
sentence:
And his
speech and mien found favour in the eyes of the Shah, and he called unto him his Wise
Men and bade them question the stars of this matter.

label: mubid | search | heaven
sentence:
Three days and three nights did the
Mubids search the heavens without ceasing, and on the fourth they came before the Shah
and spake.

label: they | say | hail
sentence:
And they said unto him-
"Hail to thee, hero of the golden girdle, for we bring unto thee glad tidings.

label: son | be | pair
sentence:
The son of
Sam and the daughter of Mihrab shall be a glorious pair, and from their union shall spring
a son like to a war-elephant, and he shall subdue all men by his sword and raise the glory
of Iran even unto the skies.

label: he | uproot | wicked
sentence:
And he shall uproot the wicked from the earth so that there
shall be no room for them.

label: segsar | feel | weight
sentence:
Segsars and Mazinderan shall feel the weight of his mace, and
he shall bring much woe upon Turan, but Iran shall be loaded with prosperity at his hands.

label: he | give | sleep
sentence:
And he will give back sleep to the unhappy, and close the doors of discord, and bar the
paths of wrong-doing.

label: kingdom | rejoice | ∅
sentence:
The kingdom will rejoice while he lives; Roum, Ind, and Iran will
grave his name upon their seals."

label: he | charge | mubids
sentence:
When the Shah had heard this he charged the Mubids that they keep secret that which
they had revealed unto him.

label: he | call | zal
sentence:
And he called for Zal that he might question him and test his
wisdom.

label: men | seat | circle
sentence:
And the Wise Men and the Mubids were seated in a circle, and they put these
questions to the son of Sam.

label: zal | ponder | ∅
sentence:
And Zal pondered a while and then answered and said-
'Twelve moons in the year, and each I praise As a new-made king on a new throne's blaze:
Each comes to an end in thirty days.

label: zal | think | horse
sentence:
"
And Zal thought again yet a while and answered-
"Two shining horses, one black, one white.

label: one | be | day
sentence:
That run for ever in rapid flight; The one is
the day, the other the night, That count the throbs of the heavens height, Like the hunted
prey from the following chase They flee, yet neither wins the race.

label: mubid | question | he
sentence:
"
Then the third Mubid questioned him and said-
"Thirty knights before the king Pass along.

label: one | be | ∅
sentence: Regard the thing Closely; one is gone.

label: thirty | be | train
sentence:
Again
Look- the thirty are in train.

label: zal | answer | ∅
sentence:
"
And Zal answered and spake-
"Thirty knights of whom the train Is full, then fails, then fills again, Know, each moon is
reckoned thus, So willed by God who governs us, And thy word is true of the faint
moon's wane, Now failing in darkness, now shining plain."

label: mubid | question | he
sentence:
Then the fourth Mubid questioned him and said-
"See a green garden full of springs; A strong man with a sickle keen Enters, and reaps
both dry and green; No word thine utmost anguish wrings.

label: pity | fear | ∅
sentence:
"
And Zal bethought him and replied-
"Thy word was of a garden green, A reaper with a sickle keen, Who cuts alike the fresh
and the dry Nor heedeth prayer nor any cry: Time is the reaper, we the grass; Pity nor
fear his spirit has, But old and young he reaps alike.

label: rank | stay | strike
sentence:
No rank can stay his sickle's strike,
No love, but he will leave it lorn, For to this end all men are born.

label: death | shut | it
sentence:
Birth opes to all the
gate of Life, Death shuts it down on love and strife, And Fate, that counts the breath of
man, Measures to each a reckoned span.

label: tree | dry | ∅
sentence:
"
Then the fifth Mubid questioned him and said-
"Look how two lofty cypresses Spring up, like reeds, from stormy seas, There builds a
bird his dwelling-place; Upon the one all night he stays, But swift, with the dawn, across
he flies; The abandoned tree dries up and dies, While that whereon he sets his feet
Breathes odours out, surpassing sweet.

label: one | be | ever
sentence:
The one is dead for ever and aye, The other lives
and blooms alway.

label: zal | bethink | he
sentence:
"
Then Zal yet again bethought him before he said-
"Hear of the sea-born cypresses, Where builds a bird, and rests, and flees.

label: sun | fly | ∅
sentence:
From the Ram
to the Scales the earth o'erpowers, Shadows obscure of the night that lowers, But when
the Scales' sign it must quit, Darkness and gloom o'ermaster it; The sides of heaven thy
fable shows Whence grief to man or blessing flows, The sun like a bird flies to and fro,
Weal with him bringing, but leaving woe.

label: mubid | question | he
sentence:
"
Then the sixth Mubid questioned him, and it was the last question that he asked, and he
deemed it the hardest of all to answer.

label: man | hang | word
sentence:
And all men hung upon his words and listened to
the answer of Zal.

label: i | find | town
sentence:
And the Mubid said-
"Builded on a rock I found A town.

label: man | leave | gate
sentence:
Men left the gate and chose A thicket on the level
ground.

label: mansion | rise | roof
sentence:
Soon their soaring mansions rose Lifting roofs that reach the moon, Some men
slaves, some kings, became, Of their earlier city soon The memory died in all.

label: earthquake | lie | land-
sentence: an earthquake; down, Lost in the chasm lies the land-

label: i | be | ∅
sentence:
Seek in thy soul the truth
of this; This before kings proclaim, I was, If rightly thou the riddle rede, Black earth to
musk thou hast changed indeed."

label: this | count | ∅
sentence:
And Zal pondered this riddle but a little while, and then opened his mouth and said-
"The eternal, final world is shown By image of a rock-built town; The thicket is our
passing life, A place of pleasure and of pain, A world of dreams and eager strife, A time
for labour, and loss, and gain; This counts thy heart-beats, at its will Prolongs their pulse
or makes it still.

label: we | leave | home
sentence:
But winds and earthquake rouse: a cry Goes up of bitterness and woe,
Now we must leave our homes below And climb the rocky fastness high.

label: ∅ | remain | ∅
sentence:
Another reaps
our fruit of pain, That yet to another leaves his gain; So was it aye, must so remain.

label: stalks | reign | we
sentence:
Well
for us if our name endure, Though we shall pass, beloved and pure, For all the evil man
hath done, Stalks, when he dies, in the sight of the sun; When dust is strown on breast
and head, Then desolation reigns with dread.

label: shah | be | ∅
sentence:
"
When Zal had spoken thus the Shah was glad, and an the assembly were amazed, and
lauded the son of Sam.

label: bade | prepare | ∅
sentence:
And the King bade a great banquet be prepared, and they drank
wine until the world was darkened, and the heads of the drinkers were troubled.

label: zal | pray | ∅
sentence:
Then
when morn was come Zal prayed that the Shah would dismiss him.

label: minuchihr | abide | i
sentence:
But Minuchihr said-
"Not so, abide with me yet another day," and he bade the drums be beaten to call together
his heroes, for he desired to test Zal also in feats of strength.

label: shah | sit | roof
sentence:
And the Shah sat upon the
roof of his house and looked down upon the games, and he beheld Zal, the son of Sam,
do mighty deeds of prowess.

label: he | shoot | arrow
sentence:
With his arrow did he shoot farther and straighter than the
rest, and with his spear he pierced all shields, and in wrestling he overcame the strongest
who had never known defeat.

label: ∅ | clap | hand
sentence:
When the nobles beheld these doughty deeds they shouted
and clapped their hands, and Minuchihr loaded Zal with gifts.

label: he | prepare | reply
sentence:
Then he prepared a reply
unto the letter of Sam.

label: he | listen | desire
sentence:
And he wrote-
"O my Pehliva, hero of great renown, I have listened to thy desires, and I have beheld the
youth who is worthy to be thy son.

label: he | find | favour
sentence:
And he hath found favour in my sight, and I send him
back to thee satisfied.

label: enemy | be | ∅
sentence: May his enemies be impotent to harm him.

label: zal | set | ∅
sentence:
"
Then when the Shah had given him leave to go, Zal set forth, and he bare his head high in
the joy of his heart.

label: sam | be | happiness
sentence:
And when he came before his father and gave to him the letter of the
Shah, Sam was young again for happiness.

label: drum | sound | signal
sentence:
Then the drums sounded the signal to depart,
and the tents were prepared, and a messenger, mounted on a fleet dromedary, was sent
unto Mihrab to tell him that Sam and Zal were setting forth for Cabul.

label: standard | deck | city
sentence:
And silken standards of bright colour decked the city, and the sounds of trumpets,
harps, and cymbals filled the air.

label: sindokht | tell | tiding
sentence:
And Sindokht told the glad tidings to Rodabeh, and they
made ready the house like unto a paradise.

label: they | lie | floor
sentence:
Carpets broidered with gold and precious
stones did they lay down upon its floors, and set forth thrones of ivory and rich carving.

label: mihrab | go | ∅
sentence:
Then when the guests were come near unto Cabul, Mihrab went forth to meet them, and
he placed upon the head of Zal a crown of diamonds, and they came into the city in
triumph.

label: people | do | homage
sentence:
And all the people did homage before them, and Sindokht met them at the doors
of the King's house, and poured out musk and precious stones before them.

label: sam | smile | ∅
sentence:
Then Sam,
when he had replied to their homage, smiled, and turned to Sindokht and said-
"How much longer dost thou think to hide Rodabeh from our eyes?

label: sindokht | say | ∅
sentence:
"
And Sindokht said, "What wilt thou give me to see the sun?"

label: i | give | thee
sentence:
Then Sam replied, "All that thou wilt, even unto my slaves and my throne, will I give to
thee."

label: sindokht | lead | he
sentence:
Then Sindokht led him within the curtains, and when Sam beheld Rodabeh he was struck
dumb with wonder, for her beauty exceeded dreams, and he knew not how he could find
words to praise her.

label: he | ask | mihrab
sentence:
Then he asked of Mihrab that he would give unto him her hand, and
they concluded an alliance according to custom and the law.

label: lover | seat | throne
sentence:
And the lovers were seated
upon a throne, and Mihrab read out the list of the gifts, and it was so long the ear did not
suffice to hear them.

label: they | repair | banquet
sentence:
Then they repaired unto the banquet, and they feasted seven days
without ceasing.

label: sam | go | ∅
sentence:
And when a month had passed Sam went back to Seistan, and Zal and
Rodabeh followed after him.

label: he | set | battle
sentence:
And speedily did he set forth again to battle, and left the
kingdom in the hands of his son, and Zal administered it with wisdom and judgment.

label: rodabeh | sit | he
sentence:
And
Rodabeh sat beside him on the throne, and he placed a crown of gold upon her head.
